text.title: Batch and prompt-cache discounts stack multiplicatively
text.summary: On Anthropic and Gemini, the 50% Batch discount composes with the 90% prompt-cache-read discount; submit async workloads via Batches with cache_control on the longest stable prefix to land at ~5% of synchronous-uncached cost.

section.title: Stacking math and provider matrix
section.p: The Batch API charges 0.5× base price and applies before the cache layer; cache-read is 0.1× base. Both factors combine multiplicatively on cached tokens, giving 0.5 × 0.1 = 0.05 of synchronous uncached input cost per batch item that hits the cache. This works on Anthropic Messages Batches and Gemini Batch Mode; OpenAI's Batch API is 50% off but explicitly does NOT stack with prompt caching.

rule.statement: For any agent workload tolerating 24h SLA, submit through the provider Batch API AND mark the longest static prefix (tools and system) with `cache_control: ephemeral` — verify the provider documents stack composition before assuming both discounts apply.
rule.rationale: Anthropic pricing page states batch + cache compose; Gemini documents stacking with implicit caching; OpenAI documents that they do not. Skipping the cache_control inside batches forfeits 90% on the dominant input bill, so the per-item cost on stable-prefix workloads is 10× higher than necessary.

example.note: System prompt and tools are byte-identical across items — first item pays cache-write (1.25x), every subsequent item lands at 0.5 * 0.1 = 0.05x of synchronous-uncached input.

example.code: # OpenAI — batch and cache do NOT stack client.batches.create(input_file_id=f.id, endpoint="/v1/chat/completions", completion_window="24h") # Each row reuses the same system prompt; you get 50% off. # Adding `prompt_cache_key` does not compose — you still get 50%, not 5%.
example.note: Designing the OpenAI pipeline as if stacking applied overestimates savings 10×; verify provider pricing docs before promising the number to the team.

antipattern.description: Embedding the timestamp, request ID, or row index inside the cached system prompt because it "feels safer."
antipattern.reason: Every item's prefix differs by 8 bytes, so every item is a cache miss; you pay 1.25× cache-write per item AND get the 50% batch discount only — net cost is HIGHER than uncached batch.

section.reference: Anthropic Batches submission with cache_control on system+tools.
Anthropic pricing — batch and cache discount composition.
Anthropic prompt caching announcement (90% read discount).
Gemini Batch Mode (50% off, stacks with implicit caching).
OpenAI Batch API — 50% discount; does not stack with prompt caching.
